text.title: Weaviate — Procedure
text.summary: 6-step: install v4 client → define schema → upsert with cross-refs → hybrid query → tune alpha → snapshot.

section.title: Step 1 — Install Weaviate + v4 client
section.p: `docker run -p 8080:8080 -p 50051:50051 cr.weaviate.io/semitechnologies/weaviate:latest` OR Weaviate Cloud cluster. `pip install "weaviate-client>=4"`.

section.title: Step 2 — Define schema (classes + properties + cross-refs)
section.p: Use `client.collections.create(name="Document", properties=[...], references=[ReferenceProperty(name="author", target_collection="Author")], vectorizer_config=..., replication_config=ReplicationConfig(factor=2))`. Apply r2 + r4.

section.title: Step 3 — Bulk import with cross-references
section.p: Use `with collection.batch.dynamic() as batch: batch.add_object(properties=..., references={...}, uuid=...)`. Apply r5 for multi-modal.

section.title: Step 4 — Hybrid query
section.p: `collection.query.hybrid(query, alpha=0.5, limit=k, return_metadata=MetadataQuery(score=True, explain_score=True))`. Apply r3.

section.title: Step 5 — Tune alpha on a representative query set
section.p: Sweep alpha ∈ {0.25, 0.5, 0.75}; pick the value with highest MRR on the eval set. Pin the result in code.

section.title: Step 6 — Snapshot before schema change
section.p: `weaviate snapshot create` (CLI) or `client.backup.create(...)`. Restore-test on staging before promoting any schema change.
